method_summary: Reflexion is formalized as an iterative optimization process where an Actor produces a trajectory τ₀ by interacting with the environment, an Evaluator computes a score rt = Me(τ₀), and a Self-Reflection model generates a verbal summary sr₀ which is stored in memory for improvement across trials.
key_innovation: The use of verbal experience feedback (self-reflection summaries) that allows language agents to learn from their own experiences across multiple trials.
limitations_stated: Memory must be bounded by maximum number of stored experiences to adhere to max context LLM limitations

performance_claim.statement: Reflexion agents significantly outperform currently widely-used decision-making approaches by utilizing self-reflection
performance_claim.metrics: Not specified in the provided text

title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
